🚨 Recruiting students and postdoc for Fall 2026!!! 🚨

The Baecher lab at Emory’s Dept. of Environmental Science

See our projects See our publications →
Welcome to the Qμest lab

The Qμest lab is led by Dr. J Alex Baecher (⬇ CV). We are an applied ecological research group, broadly interested in the processes that determine the distribution of biodiversity in space and time. Our work integrates field studies, laboratory experiments, big data, cutting-edge modeling techniques, and high-performance computing to address questions related to conservation, invasions, disease, and global change.

🚨We are currently recruiting!🚨

  • Fall 2026: 2-3 undergraduate researchers
  • Fall 2026: 1 postdoctoral research associate
  • Spring 2027: 1 PhD student

Interested? Take a look at some of our research projects and publications below. To inquire about opportunities, reach out to Alex ✉️.

Research
Appalachian Salamander Ecology

Natural History, Ecology, and Conservation of Plethodontid Salamanders.

Jan 15, 2026
Biodiversity redistribution

Understanding the ecology of climate-induced range shifts

Jan 10, 2026
Reptile and Amphibian Ecology

Natural History, Ecology, and Conservation of Herps in the Southeastern US.

Jan 5, 2026
Forecasting ecological dynamics and disease

Understanding the pattern and process behind ecological phenomena, including population changes, invasive species, and vector-borne disease.

Mar 15, 2025
Integrated population modeling

Integrated field data with semi-structured citizen science data to model population dynamics

Feb 15, 2025
